patch.value: Build package-first pm ecosystem and install command

Rename the pm extension ecosystem toward packages, restore pm install as the user-facing package installer, and model Pi-style packages that can bundle extensions, SDK integrations, prompts, skills, themes, and docs.

Pi package model researched and documented in tracker; pm package/install plan decomposed into child work; pm install can install managed package sources while preserving extension compatibility; SDK/docs/tests updated; full CLI/SDK dogfood passes in a temporary project; telemetry and Sentry verification are recorded.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate check before create: ran pm context --limit 10,pm search packages extensions SDK install --limit 10,pm list-open --limit 20,and pm list-in-progress --limit 20. Existing matches were closed extension/SDK/install work plus open parent pm-rnpb; no active item covered package-first ecosystem rename and pm install restoration,so this child feature is net-new under pm-rnpb.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Pi package research snapshot 2026-05-11: pi.dev/packages advertises npm-published package catalog entries and installs with pi install npm:<package>. Current Pi docs define packages as bundles of extensions, skills, prompt templates, and themes; package.json pi manifest supports extensions/skills/prompts/themes arrays plus gallery image/video; conventional directories are auto-discovered if no pi manifest exists. Sources include npm, git URLs/shorthands, and local paths; project-local installs use -l and persist to .pi/settings.json; temporary try-run uses -e. Pi package manager dedupes project over global, supports filtering per resource type, pins versioned npm/git refs, runs npm install for npm/git packages, and loads resources through a central resource-loader.

patch.value:
Package ecosystem plan after Pi research:

1. pm-9x1c in progress: ship package-first command aliases and pm install, including local package roots, GitHub package roots, npm:<spec> package roots, pm.extensions/pi.extensions, conventional extension directories, contracts, native Pi, MCP, docs, and tests.
2. pm-t5ud open: design the full package manifest/resource model for extensions, skills, prompts, themes, docs, commands, and SDK resources.
3. pm-su6i open: implement package resource loading beyond extension manifests, with project/global precedence and diagnostics.
4. pm-lwun open: complete package-first terminology migration in help, docs, skills, examples, telemetry naming, and UX while keeping pm extension compatibility.
5. pm-2b3l open: define marketplace/gallery metadata and package discovery, informed by pi.dev/packages npm keyword/catalog behavior.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Implementation slice completed: child pm-9x1c closed after package-first aliases, pm install, package-root/npm install support, SDK/Pi/MCP contracts, docs/skills, manual temp-project dogfood, full release gates, and observability checks. Roadmap children remain open for the broader Pi-style package ecosystem: manifest/resource model (pm-t5ud), resource loader for skills/prompts/themes/docs (pm-su6i), package gallery metadata (pm-2b3l), and full terminology migration (pm-lwun).

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: External research refresh 2026-05-11: Pi packages bundle extensions, skills, prompts, and themes; support npm/git/local sources; run npm install for npm/git packages; use package.json pi manifests or convention directories; support per-resource filters, project-over-global dedupe, enable/disable controls, and explicit security warnings. OpenClaw separates small core from npm/community plugins, supports local/archive/npm/marketplace installs, list/inspect/doctor/enable/disable, discovery precedence, plugin slots, manifest-declared capabilities, and focused SDK subpath imports. Implication for pm: keep item/storage/history/config/output/package loader primitives in core; move optional workflows/resources/integrations behind installable packages with manifest contracts and diagnostics.

Main pm CLI/SDK must not contain direct Pi agent integration or agent-bundle resource kinds such as prompts/skills/MCPs. Pivoting implementation to remove direct Pi coupling from core code and keep packages focused on general project-management extensions/resources.

patch.value:
Package ecosystem plan after Pi research:\n\n1. pm-9x1c in progress: ship package-first command aliases and pm install, including local package roots, GitHub package roots, npm:<spec> package roots, pm.extensions/pi.extensions, conventional extension directories, contracts, native Pi, MCP, docs, and tests.\n2. pm-t5ud open: design the full package manifest/resource model for extensions, skills, prompts, themes, docs, commands, and SDK resources.\n3. pm-su6i open: implement package resource loading beyond extension manifests, with project/global precedence and diagnostics.\n4. pm-lwun open: complete package-first terminology migration in help, docs, skills, examples, telemetry naming, and UX while keeping pm extension compatibility.\n5. pm-2b3l open: define marketplace/gallery metadata and package discovery, informed by pi.dev/packages npm keyword/catalog behavior.

Package-first architecture plan, 2026-05-11 refresh:
1. Keep bare core limited to item storage/format, history, locking, settings, output, command runtime, lifecycle primitives, SDK contracts, and package loader/governance.
2. Treat search providers/vector stores, import/export bridges, calendar presentations, agent-harness adapters, docs/skills/plugins, and other workflow-specific behavior as package candidates unless they are required to manage pm items without packages.
3. Preserve the SDK by keeping neutral capability contracts public, adding narrow package manifest contracts, and moving provider/custom helper code into package-owned APIs instead of core SDK exports.
4. Implement vertical slices: first-package extraction for Beads/Todos, terminology/docs migration, package catalog metadata, then optional search/calendar/observability package boundaries.
5. Verify every slice with sandboxed PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH tests, temp-project manual installs via pm install, contracts/health/package doctor, full coverage, telemetry/Sentry checks, commit/push, and green GitHub Actions.

author: codex-agent

patch.value:
Package-first architecture plan after external package-manager references:

1. Keep bare pm core limited to project-management primitives: item storage/format, history, locking, settings, output, command runtime, lifecycle commands, SDK contracts, package loading, governance, and minimal health.
2. Treat workflow-specific behavior as optional packages unless it is required to manage pm items without packages. First delivered slice: pm-hxp2 extracted Beads/Todos import/export bridges into installable first-party package roots.
3. Keep follow-up package work provider-neutral and pm-specific: pm-t5ud for manifest/package contract design, pm-su6i for pm package resource loading, pm-lwun for package-first docs/UX terminology, and pm-2b3l for package gallery metadata.
4. Later package-boundary candidates include templates, search providers/vector stores, calendar presentations, telemetry/Sentry adapters, and external bridge workflows after their core contracts are narrowed.
5. Verification protocol for each slice: sandboxed PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH tests, temp-project pm install dogfood, contracts/health/package doctor, full coverage, observability checks, commit/push, and green GitHub Actions.

Build a package-first pm ecosystem around installable project-management packages while using Pi/OpenClaw only as architecture references for npm/git/local package sourcing, manifest contracts, diagnostics, and small-core extensibility.

value.text: Detailed package extraction plan from 2026-05-14 codebase audit: 1) pm-calendar: move calendar/cal views, reminders/events/recurrence expansion, and schedule presets behind package commands while keeping item deadline fields core. 2) pm-search-advanced: keep keyword search core, move semantic/hybrid reindex, embedding providers, vector stores, cache refresh, and advanced search provider defaults to installable search packages. 3) pm-linked-test-runner: keep linked test metadata core, move subprocess execution, background runs, assertion policies, PM-context sandboxing, and test-runs lifecycle to a runner package. 4) pm-governance-audit: move dedupe-audit, comments-audit, normalize sweeps, advanced validate checks, and broad update-many governance workflows to a governance package. 5) pm-guide-shell: keep help core, move guide topics, completion generation, and shell-specific UX to a docs/shell package. 6) pm-templates: move templates save/list/show and create --template acceleration into a templates package. Cross-cutting requirement: every extraction must preserve SDK primitives, dynamic contracts/MCP availability, package TypeScript sources, temp-project dogfood, and release/observability gates.

value.text: Package/changelog lane evidence 2026-05-24: GitHub issue #53 is NOT reproducible on current main when using node dist/cli.js in a clean temp project with isolated PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH. Repro command sequence: init --defaults, install npm:pm-changelog --project, package doctor --project --detail deep --json, contracts --command 'changelog generate' --flags-only --json, create closed seed item, changelog generate --stdout. Result: installed pm-changelog@2026.5.24-6, package doctor ok with runtime_active_total=1 and activation_status ok=1, contracts exposed changelog generate, generated changelog included the seeded closed item. Published @unbrained/pm-cli latest is still 2026.5.18, matching issue #53's published-CLI repro; fix is a pm-cli installer/runtime-dependency fix already present on main and needs release propagation, not a pm-changelog code fix.

value.author: codex-subagent-github
value.text: Evidence 2026-05-25 codex-subagent-github: GitHub issue #53 (pm install npm package does not install runtime dependencies for extension SDK imports) appears covered in this checkout. src/cli/commands/extension.ts resolves packed npm package sources, extracts the tarball, then runs installNpmPackageRuntimeDependencies(packageRoot), which installs dependencies/optionalDependencies/peerDependencies with npm install --ignore-scripts --no-audit --package-lock=false --no-save before extension discovery. Existing integration coverage tests an npm package extension importing @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk. Verification: gh issue view 53; rg @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk; PM_AUTHOR=codex-subagent-github node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts -t 'installs runtime dependencies for packed npm package extensions' passed (1 passed, 74 skipped).

value.author: codex-full-audit
value.text: SDK/package extensibility remediation from full audit: bundled packages had drift between extension manifest.json and module manifest capabilities, optional package commands leaked through PM_CORE_COMMAND_NAMES/static flag contract resolution, and package runtimes deep-loaded dist/sdk/runtime without a public export. This pass aligned pm-search-advanced capabilities, removed optional package-owned commands from the core SDK enum/static resolver, added a regression asserting the boundary, and published ./sdk/runtime in package exports with a package-manifest regression. Remaining best-practice follow-ups: documented extension lifecycle/deactivate semantics and fuller package-author docs.

value.author: codex-sdk-calendar-review
value.text: SDK/package architecture review 2026-05-25: current dirty changes preserve the package-first boundary by removing stale optional package-owned commands from static PM_CORE_COMMAND_NAMES/static flag resolution and publishing ./sdk/runtime in package exports. Remaining architecture concern for future package authoring: bundled package runtime loaders still dynamically locate dist/sdk/runtime.js via PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT; this is workable for bundled packages and now guarded by export tests, but a cleaner next SDK step would be a documented stable package-runtime loader helper/subpath so package authors do not copy env-root dynamic import boilerplate across calendar/search/governance/guide/test runtimes.

value.author: manual-dogfood-specialist
value.text: Manual dogfood evidence 2026-05-25: package-first/extensibility path worked in a clean isolated project. `pm init --with-packages` installed 8 bundled managed packages; `pm package explore` showed active calendar/governance/guide/linked-test/search-advanced/templates/todos/beads packages; `pm package init ./packages/pm-launchops-helper` scaffolded package.json, manifest, index.js, README; `pm install ./packages/pm-launchops-helper --project` activated it; `pm pm-launchops-helper ping --json` returned ok; `pm install npm:pm-changelog --project` activated pm-changelog@2026.5.24-15 and `changelog generate --stdout` produced a valid changelog. `pm package doctor --project --detail summary` reported 10 active managed extensions with no warnings.

value.author: sdk-calendar-search-subagent
value.text: SDK/package/calendar/search architecture review evidence 2026-05-25: package-first boundary is mostly preserved in current dirty checkout. Positive checks: package.json now exports ./sdk/runtime; bundled package module capabilities are aligned by package-manifest regression; calendar package validates invalid --format/conflicting --json+--format before running calendar core and returns usage exit 2 for repeated positional views; search-advanced JS manifest now includes schema capability; LanceDB local snapshot cache now stats snapshot mtime/size and reloads when another process deletes or rewrites backing JSON. Focused tests passed: pnpm test -- --run tests/unit/calendar-command.spec.ts tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts tests/unit/schema-command.spec.ts tests/unit/sdk-index.spec.ts tests/unit/vector-store-adapter.spec.ts (73 tests); pnpm test -- --run tests/unit/completion-command.spec.ts tests/unit/contracts-command.spec.ts (109 tests). Isolated runtime smoke with PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH temp + pm init --defaults --with-packages verified runtime contracts for reindex/completion and calendar markdown output. Finding to carry in package-first work: shell completion generation is still partly static/hardcoded and not package/runtime-contract driven end to end. Bash top-level command list comes from PM_CORE_COMMAND_NAMES and omits installed package commands, while zsh/fish descriptions still unconditionally advertise package commands such as calendar/reindex/guide/completion. Bash accepts runtime field flags for only list/create/update/update-many/search/calendar/context; zsh/fish do not consume runtime command_flags. Recommendation: make completion derive command catalog, package command availability, aliases, and flags from runtime contracts/active registrations, then render all shells from the same contract model.

This confirms the current package-first flow is functional for local package creation/install smoke.
